sections.paragraph:
with suff. בִּלְעָדַי, בִּלְעָדֶיךָ (comp. of בַּל not, and עַד, עֲדֵי until).

(1) pr. not unto, nothing to, a particle of deprecating or declining. Gen 14:24, הַנְּעָרִים בִּלְעָדַי רַק אֲשֶׁר אָֽבְלוּ “nothing (shall come) to me;” I claim nothing, “only what the young men have eaten,” etc. Gen 41:16, בִּלְעָדַי אֱלֹהִים יַעֲנֶה אֶת־שְׁלֹום פַּרְעֹה “(It is) not I; God will answer as to the welfare of Pharaoh.”

(2) without. Gen 41:44, “without thee (without thy knowledge and consent) no one shall lift up his hand.”

(3) besides, Isa 45:6. Ellipt. for בִּלְעֲדֵי אֲשֶׁר besides that which. Job 34:32, בִּלְעֲדֵי אֶחֱזֶה אַתָּה הֹורֵנִי “(if I have sinned) besides the things which I see, show it to me.” Syr. ܒܶܥܠܳܕ, ܒܶܥܠܳܕ ܡܶܢ id.
